title: Machine-guided path sampling to discover mechanisms of molecular self-organization
methods: committor learning via pyramidal ResNet (negative log-likelihood)
symbolic regression with genetic algorithm (dcgpy, Pareto-front)
hierarchical trajectory clustering with dynamic time warping
data_sources: Code Ocean capsule (AIMMD code + LiCl training data, ref. 72)
Zenodo repository (all remaining simulation data, ref. 73)
Pre-existing methane-clathrate TPS dataset (ref. 21, 2225 transition paths)

data_scope.systems: 4 molecular systems: ion pairing (6 species: LiCl/LiI/NaCl/NaI/CsCl/CsI), methane-clathrate nucleation, 128-bead polymer folding, Mga2 transmembrane homodimer
data_scope.data_availability: open (Code Ocean ref. 72 + Zenodo ref. 73 + GitHub)

core_conclusion: An autonomous closed-loop algorithm (AIMMD) integrates deep learning with transition path theory to sample rare molecular self-organization events and learn interpretable committor-based mechanisms via symbolic regression. Applied to four diverse systems, it achieves ~10x speedup over standard TPS, reveals many-body solvent effects and competing nucleation/dimerization pathways, and transfers mechanistic models across thermodynamic states and chemical space with minimal additional data.
why_it_matters: Closes the sampling-learning loop for rare molecular events at 10x speedup; delivers interpretable physics (compact analytical expressions) from high-dimensional deep learning; demonstrates cross-domain generality across ion solvation, crystal nucleation, polymer folding, and membrane-protein assembly; enables chemical-space transfer with only 1000 additional labeled examples per new system.
why_nature: General-purpose algorithmic contribution deployable across molecular simulation problems; rigorously couples AI with transition path theory for physically validated models; addresses the interpretability gap in scientific ML via symbolic regression; fully open code and data.
added_value_to_my_research: GVP-GNN committor architecture -> graph-learned distance-decay for physics-informed R2SFCA accessibility
Symbolic regression Pareto-front -> interpretable closed-form spatial accessibility indicators from ensemble ML
Variational path-ensemble loss on partial trajectories -> robust spatial interaction learning from censored GPS mobility data
Few-shot transfer learning (1000 shots) -> label-efficiency benchmark for fine-tuning geospatial foundation models
Dynamic time warping TP clustering -> competing-pathway detection in job-housing flow networks and commuting-regime discovery

related.title: Learning the committor without collective variables
related.relation: GVP-GNN architecture that learns the committor directly from atomic coordinates without hand-crafted features — natural successor to AIMMD's ResNet backbone

related.title: Iterative variational learning of committor-consistent transition pathways using artificial neural networks
related.relation: variational loss extension for committor learning from path ensembles — directly extends AIMMD's self-consistency framework

related.title: Stop explaining black box machine learning models for high stakes decisions and use interpretable models instead
related.relation: foundational argument for interpretable models over black-box explanations — theoretical grounding for AIMMD's symbolic-regression design choice

related.title: Molecular deep learning at the edge of chemical space
related.relation: uncertainty-aware out-of-distribution generalization in molecular ML — enables active-learning extension of AIMMD's chemical-space transfer

related.title: Organic reaction mechanism classification using machine learning
related.relation: ML classification of competing molecular mechanisms from fingerprints — complementary to AIMMD's pathway-clustering for multi-mechanism systems

figures.caption: Learning the assembly mechanism of ions in water: the AIMMD loop, self-consistency check, committor validation, and transfer learning across ionic species
Interpretable multi-ion model: input relevance for 179 coordinates, Pareto plot of symbolic regression candidates, and a single expression valid for six monovalent ion species
Methane-clathrate nucleation: molecular snapshots, committor validation, input importance (T, n_w, n_c), and temperature-dependent mechanistic switch
Polymer folding: committor heat map as a function of potential energy U and crystallinity Q6, with molecular snapshots at p_B = 0, 0.5, 1
Mga2 transmembrane dimer assembly: snapshots, self-consistency, committor validation, and hierarchical TP clustering revealing two competing dimerization pathways
